title: Walang Sugat
creator: Dennis Faustino
description: A romantic drama detailing the forbidden love between Tenyong and Julia, which is hindered by her mother's desire for her to marry a rich man, amidst the backdrop of war and national liberation
subject: Theater

title: Malvarosa
creator: Dr. Gregorio Fernandez
description: A dramatic thunderbolt that won Rebecca del Rio the Golden Harvest Award in the 5th Asian Film Festival. Here is a poverty stricken family who live right by the railroad tracks on the wrong side of them not by choice, but because destiny has willed it.
subject: Film

title: Ang Paglalakbay ni Radiya Mangandiri
creator: Soxy Topacio
description: Subtitled Isang Pilipinong Ramayana, was based on an old Maranao version of the Indian epic. Peta production of it underlined the Philippines ancient cultural connections to the Asian heartland. According to scholar and playwright Nicanor Tiongson, Through imaginative use of ethnic movements and music, Peta Mangandiri succeeded in raising crucial questions about the nature and direction of leadership, seven years after the fall of the Philippine Lawana, the Marcos dictatorship in 1986.
subject: Theater

title: Ang Larawan
creator: Rolando Tinio
description: Rolando Tinio’s swan song was this play, a musical version of Nick Joaquin’s A Portrait of the Artist as Filipino, generally regarded as the most important Filipino play, now set to Ryan Cayabyab’s soaring, melancholy melodies, with translation and direction duties by Tinio himself. Celeste Legaspi played Candida, Zsa Zsa Padilla was Paula and Ricky Davao was Tony Javier; also in the cast were Hajji Alejandro, Louie Reyes, Fides Cuyugan Asencio, Armida Siguion-Reyna, Dawn Zulueta, Tinio died a few days before Larawan opening, making this essential musical his last masterwork.
subject: Theater

title: Dalagang Bukid
creator: Felix "Nonon" Padilla
description: The collapse of the old order ushered in new configurations at Imelda’s old playground, the Cultural Center of the Philippines. The erstwhile resident theater companies, Rolando Tinio Teatro Pilipino and Tony Espejo’s Bulwagang Gantimpala, were eased out, replaced by a new company Tanghalang Pilipino, with Nonon Padilla as artistic director. TP inaugural show was a revival of the sarsuwela Dalagang Bukid, which reminded the public about pioneering sarsuwela queen Atang de la Rama, also the Philippines’ first film actress; that year, she would be conferred the National Artist Award for Theater.
subject: Theater

title: Balweg
creator: Butch Perez
description: A biographical action film centering on the life of Father Conrado Balweg and the revolutionary struggles in the Cordillera.
subject: Film

title: Minsa'y Isang Gamu-Gamo
creator: Soxy Tapacio
description: The 1976 Lupita Kashiwahara film, touching on the US bases and pervasive American influence, was transformed by Peta into a play that marked the theater debut of the movie’s original star, Nora Aunor at about the same time that the Philippine Senate was deliberating on and making the historic vote to kick out the American military presence in the country.
subject: Theater
location: Raha Sulayman Theater, Fort Santiago, Maynila

title: Noli Me Tangere
creator: Felix "Nonon" Padilla
description: An operatic musical tracing the tragic homecoming of Crisostomo Ibarra, an idealistic young reformist who returns to the Philippines after studying in Europe. He seeks to build a modern school for his townspeople and marry his childhood sweetheart, Maria Clara. However, his noble efforts are systematically crushed by the tyrannical Friar Damaso, who frames Ibarra for an orchestrated anti-colonial rebellion. Stripped of his wealth, excommunicated from the church, and forced into a dangerous river escape with the fugitive Elias, Ibarra is left for dead. Maria Clara, believing her lover is killed and forced to hide a scandalous family secret, refuses an arranged marriage and locks herself away permanently inside a secluded convent.
subject: Theater

creator: Soxy Topacio
description: Peta’s thundering musical production, music by Lucien Letaba, libretto by Charley de la Paz, staged in the run-up to the Philippine centennial celebrations in 1998 and rerun a number of times after, starred Rody Vera as Andres Bonifacio, Ariel Rivera as Emilio Jacinto and Bodjie Pascua as Emilio Aguinaldo. It was, in the words of political commentator Manuel L. Quezon III, the highlight of the Centennial celebrations and a musical I wish would be constantly performed.
subject: Theater
location: UP Theater, UP Diliman Campus, Quezon City

title: Rama at Sita
creator: Leo Rialp
description: An independent production, Rama at Sita is still remembered by many theatergoers as the most lavish theater production they have ever seen. The musical, which combined the Cayabyab-Lumbera libretto from Ballet Philippines’ “Rama, Hari” with new music by Danny Tan, Roy Iglesias and Dodjie Simon, spared no expense, from its opulent costumes and sets. At one point, a full-size elephant ambled its way to the stage carrying a resplendent Rama, played by Ariel Rivera. To its star-studded cast that included Lani Misalucha, Raymond Lauchengco, Robert Seña, Jaya, Nonie Buencamino, Franco Laurel, Pinky Marquez.
subject: Theater
location: University Theater, University of the Philippines (UP), Diliman, Quezon City

title: Sakay
creator: Raymond Red
description: An acclaimed historical film depicting the life of Macario Sakay, a general who fought in the Philippine-American War.
subject: Film

title: Matandang Dalaga
creator: Olive La Torre
description: Carmen was about to get married and was on her way to church when Rosa Mia begged her not to go ahead with the wedding because Carmen was going to marry her husband, but the latter still insisted on his love for her fiancé until a letter was delivered saying that the wedding was no longer going ahead. Since then, Carmen has become so stubborn and distrustful of men that she even hinders the love between her sister Myrna Delgado and Ramon Revilla. Serialized in Bulaklak Magazine.
subject: Film

title: Balisong
creator: Conrado Conde
description: A deadly weapon in the hand of a fiery woman only one man could tame it’s fast-streaking fury.
subject: Film

title: Sa Piling ng mga Sugapa
creator: Gil Portes
description: A gritty social drama tackling contemporary domestic issues, directed by acclaimed filmmaker Gil Portes.
subject: Film

title: Hihintayin Kita
creator: Artemio B. Tecson
description: A solider joins the guerilla and is tasked to woe the daughter of a man who has ties with Japanese forces and and gather intel but he falls in love with her. They both get married but the woman and her father gets into an accident, so the man leaves for Manila and falls in love with doctor. Fate lead the doctor and the woman to meet.
subject: Film

title: Bata, Bata Pa'no Ka Ginawa?
creator: Chito S. Roño
description: Based on the acclaimed novel of Lualhati Bautista, this film looks into the life of women's rights advocate Lea Bustamante (Vilma Santos), who has two children from different fathers. She finds herself at a crossroads when her children's fathers propose taking their kids with them. She then decides to let her children choose who they want to stay with. Released in 1998 and directed by Chito S. Roño.
subject: Film

title: Hubad na Bayani
creator: Robert Arevalo
description: Hubad na Bayani explores the world of peasant revolts, cult organizations, talismans, and the fight for socio-economic reforms. The story revolves around Ruben a peasant who dreams of saving enough money to pay for the treatment of his sick mother, the education of his sister Pilar, as well as his own marriage to Luisa. His modest dreams are never realized as he and his family are exploited, harassed and victimized by the greedy landowners and loan sharks.
subject: Film

title: Prinsesa Gusgusin
creator: Carlos Vander Tolosa
description: It’s a fairy tale romance when a modern Cinderella meets her handsome lover prince.
subject: Film

title: Katy The Musical
creator: Nestor U. Torre
description: The work of a raft of top-flight talent, music by Ryan Cayabyab, libretto by Jose Javier Reyes, direction by Nestor Torre, and lead performances by Mitch Valdes, Celeste Legaspi, Marco Sison and Bernardo Bernardo Katy! celebrated the life and legacy of bodabil queen Katy de la Cruz. The show, hailed by then Inquirer columnist (later editor in chief) Letty Jimenez Magsanoc as having created the prototype for the Filipino musical, birthed the now-classic anthem Minsan ang Minahal ay Ako.
subject: Theater

title: Maynila: Sa mga Kuko ng Liwanag
creator: Lino Brocka
description: Julio Madiaga, a simple fisherman from the province, travels to Manila to find Ligaya, the woman he loves, after she went away with a mysterious woman promising a better future in the City. When he arrives, he becomes immersed in the city lifestyle and gets involved with its inhabitants experiencing extreme poverty, hard luck, and the overbearing need to grind for daily sustention. While Julio relentlessly searches for Ligaya, the city changes him little by little, becoming like an animal doomed to live only for survival in a wild jungle with no way out.
subject: Film

title: Tumbalik na Daigdig
creator: Tony Arnaldo
description: A story about a young architect and his friend who goes to the province to build a church Upon their arrival they notice something peculiar, it seems that the place was primarily inhabited by women they soon learn that it was due to most men enlisted and perished in the war. Lacking the manpower to build the church he started to hire women as construction workers, he then meets this particular woman.
subject: Film

title: Kundiman ng Puso
creator: Tony Cayado
description: A musical-romance movie classic tracking dramatic domestic encounters, created under Sampaguita Pictures.
subject: Film

title: Colegiala
creator: Eduardo de Castro
description: A rare pre-war studio drama exploring the social pressures faced by a young woman from a traditional rural family who moves to Manila to attend an elite convent girls' college (colegiala). She finds herself caught between her family's strict provincial expectations and the fast-paced, modern lifestyles of her wealthy urban classmates. The central conflict arises when she falls in love with an artistic young man from a lower social standing, causing her family to threaten to pull her out of school. Through perseverance and the support of her progressive teachers, she bridges the cultural divide, proving that modern education can coexist with traditional values.
subject: Film

title: Paglipas ng Dilim
creator: Rolando Tinio
description: A modern theater revival of the classic 1920 sarswela focusing on Ricardo, an idealistic young medical student who falls deeply in love with Estrella, a modest, pure-hearted seamstress. Their romance is threatened by Caridad, an aggressive, wealthy socialite who uses financial bribery and malicious lies to convince Ricardo's family that Estrella is unfaithful. Misunderstandings multiply during an elegant town fiesta, driving the young lovers apart. The truth is exposed during a climactic medical emergency where Estrella's selflessness saves a life, breaking through Caridad's deception and allowing Ricardo to publicly reunite with her as the metaphorical darkness over their lives finally passes.
subject: Theater

title: Ang Kiri
creator: Servando de los Angeles
description: A classic sarswela play following or focusing on any flirty, high-society woman named Kiri, who takes pleasure in playing with the hearts of wealthy suitors while evading any real marital commitment. Her care-free lifestyle catches up with her when she falls genuinely in love with an honorable man who rejects her advances, disgusted by her shallow lifestyle and manipulative reputation. This rejection sparks a profound emotional crisis, forcing Kiri to abandon her malicious games and undergo a quiet journey of self-reflection. She eventually wins his respect and love through genuine humility and acts of community service, transforming her public image.
subject: Theater

title: Virginia
creator: Nemesio E. Caravana
description: Virginia (1949) is a melodrama with predictable themes and stereotyped characters seen in most movies from the big studios that were now slowly becoming film factories. People from Manila are rich and haughty while the people from the province are poor, simple, but live dignified lives. The story is set during the latter part of the Pacific war, when the Americans began their offensive to take back the country from the Japanese. The rich family of Doña Tentay (Naty Bernardo) and her son Reynaldo (Armando Goyena), together with his cousin Mameng (Rosa Rosal) evacuate Manila and move to the province to escape the incessant bombing. There they encounter the pretty provincial lass, Virginia (Tessie Quintana), a corn vendor and Reynaldo and Virginia fall in love. Virginia and her father (Florentino Ballecer) are also secretly hiding an American officer from the Japanese, a Mr. Powell. Mameng is also in love with Reynaldo and tries her best to belittle and humiliate Virginia. At the war’s end, they return to Manila and Reynaldo is forced by an unexpected turn of events to marry Mameng. But Mameng is a spoiled society girl who not only dissipates Reynaldo’s fortune but is also unfaithful. Reynaldo loses his money and is forced to work as a taxi driver. One day he meets an accident and loses his sight. In the meantime, Mr. Powell, the American officer returns to the province and leaves all his property to Virginia. The roles are now reversed. Virginia is rich while Reynaldo and Mameng are dirt poor. Virginia learns about Reynaldo’s miserable situation and secretly has him cured. He regains his sight and confronts Mameng and her lover (Jaime Castelvi). A fight ensues and Mameng’s lover is accidentally killed. Reynaldo takes the blame but at the trial, a thoroughly repentant Mameng confesses to her role in this tragedy and takes poison. Reynaldo returns to the province and is reunited with Virginia.
subject: Film

title: Sumpaan
creator: Susana C. de Guzman
description: Three brothers make a vow on their father’s deathbed to be devoted to one another. As they each grow up in their own ways, they struggle to retain their bond as adults.
subject: Film

title: Orasang Ginto
creator: Manuel Conde
description: Historically significant as the first feature film produced in the Philippines immediately following the destruction of World War II. The plot details the harrowing struggles of a war-weary soldier who returns to the ruined streets of Manila to search for his displaced family. His only keepsake is a gold watch (orasang ginto) given to him by a dying comrade on the battlefield. As he navigates the city's black markets and lawless post-war slums, the watch is stolen by a desperate gang of orphans. His quest to retrieve the watch leads him to the children's hiding place, where he unexpectedly discovers his own younger sister leading them, shifting his mission from individual survival to collective healing.
subject: Film

title: La Vida de Jose Rizal
creator: Edward M. Gross
description: Based on Edward Gross’ 1905 stage play. It begins with a view of the Philippines prior to Rizal’s birth and traces his career in Europe, his exile in Dapitan, his imprisonment in Fort Santiago and his martyrdom at Bagumbayan.
subject: Film

title: El Fusilamiento de Jose Rizal
creator: Albert Yearsley
description: A film depicting the shooting of Dr. Jose Rizal, consisting of a single scene worth 500 feet of film.
subject: Film

title: Tunay na Ina
creator: Octavio Silos
description: Magdalena (Rosario Moreno) becomes pregnant because she was raped by Antonio (Exequiel Segovia). Before his death, her father (Precioso Palma) gives up the baby for adoption, thinking that no man can ever understand her past. Magdalena is engaged to Roberto (Rudy Concepcion), a decent, well-off young man. Haunted by her conscience, Magdalena writes Roberto a letter, admitting her past. Her aunt, afraid that the wedding may be called off, intercepts the letter and hides it. Assuming that Roberto has accepted her in spite of her past, she marries him. Magdalena later becomes increasingly aware that Roberto does not know the truth about her. She tries to locate her lost child. Eventually, she sees a child that she thinks is her missing daughter. The child, Tita (Tita Duran), recognizes Aling Andang (Naty Bernardo) as her mother. The story of how Tita came to Andang matches the story of how Magdalena's baby was given away. Magdalena is now sure that Tita is her child. Meanwhile, Antonio comes back and blackmails Magdalena. When Magdalena later refuses to give him more money, Antonio tells Roberto the truth. Roberto throws Magdalena out of the house. She goes to Andang and Tita to spend time with her daughter, not knowing that Junior, her son by Roberto is gravely ill. Junior dies. Magdalena is only redeemed in Roberto's eyes when the aunt produces Magdalena's letter. After Andang almost comes to death on Christmas Eve, Magdalena and Roberto decide to adopt both Tita and Aling Andang into their home.
subject: Film

title: Tulisan
creator: Jose De Villa
description: This film is about the warring women bandits. Roberta leads a group that will rescue her kidnapped twin-sister, Senyang, from Fernanda’s group.
subject: Film

title: Kerubin
creator: Octavio Silos
description: St. Peter is exasperated with the antics of Kerubin, a mischievous little angel. So he sends Kerubin to Earth with the proviso that she cannot return to heaven until she makes one sad soul happy. Kerubin settles on Anastacis, a young housewife who wants to have a child but cannot and whose husband is beginning to have a wandering eye.
subject: Film

title: Anak ng Espada
creator: Tony Cayado
description: A 1954 Philippine action-fantasy film. Directed by Tony Cayado and written by Fernando B. Monleon, this swashbuckling epic features a young cast of notable stars, including Tessie Agana, Norma Vales, and Rita Gomez. Amidst mystical swordplay and classic Filipino folklore.
subject: Film

title: Octavia
creator: Rose Mia
description: A generation feud between three stars.
subject: Film

title: Anino ni Bathala
creator: Conrado Conde
description: Earthly love or the love of god? which must one choose in order to live in his shadow?
subject: Film

title: La India Elegante y El Negrito Amante
creator: Francisco Balagtas
description: A popular 19th-century musical farce (saynete) that serves as a satirical commentary on racial, cultural, and social status. The story revolves around Kapitan Toming, a short, dark Aeta chieftain ("el negrito amante") who falls deeply in love with A popular 19th-century musical farce (saynete) that serves as a satirical commentary on racial, cultural, and social status. The story revolves around Kapitan Toming, a short, dark Aeta chieftain ("el negrito amante") who falls deeply in love with Menangge, a disdainful, sophisticated Tagalog woman ("la india elegante"). Desperate to win her affections, Toming continuously attempts to mask his indigenous identity by dressing in various elaborate European outfits, including an American frak and formal coat (lebita), under the mistaken belief that high-status foreign clothes will change his social standing. Menangge repeatedly rejects his advances based on his race. The comedic friction develops through Toming's dialogues with his companion Uban, eventually leading to a dramatic realization of self-worth and a profound message regarding local identity, discrimination, and the vanity of colonial assimilation.
subject: Theater
location: Teatro de Tondo in the district of Tondo, Manila

title: Minda Mora
creator: Severino Reyes
description: A monumental three-act Tagalog sarswela that explores the intersection of romance, religious identity, and social duty. The story focuses on the star-crossed relationship between Felix, a Christian man, and Minda, a Muslim Moro woman from Mindanao. After a deep romance develops between them, Minda becomes pregnant. Felix faces immense domestic and societal pressure due to the deep-seated cultural divides between Christian and Muslim communities at the turn of the century. The drama peaks as Felix faces a severe moral crossroad over his path in life. The storyline resolves through a powerful social message reflecting Filipino values, emphasizing a man's moral responsibility to honor and marry the woman he impregnated regardless of cultural biases, ending with a plea for domestic fidelity, integrity, and honor.
subject: Theater

title: Sa Pusod ng Dagat
creator: Marilou Diaz-Abaya
description: On an island in the 1950s, young Pepito assists his mother as she goes around practicing her trade as the only local midwife left in the village.  He gets involved in the lives and passions of other inhabitants whose beliefs, struggles and aspirations lead him to an understanding of himself and the role he must play in the life of his fellow villagers.  In a story inflected with flashes of magic realism, the remote island becomes a mirror of the wider world.
subject: Film

title: Orosman at Zafira
creator: Francisco Balagtas
description: A monumental four-part poetic komedya (moro-moro) that breaks traditional genre molds by focusing entirely on internal conflicts and the senselessness of tribal warfare. The storyline ignites when Boulasem, the power-hungry chieftain of the Tedenst tribe, disrupts a diplomatic feast to brutally assassinate Sultan Mahamud of Marruecos, plunging three independent kingdoms into war. Caught in the crossfire are the star-crossed lovers Orosman (Boulasem's honorable son) and Zafira (Mahamud’s fierce, vengeance-seeking daughter). The narrative follows the complex psychological trials of the lovers as Orosman struggles between his devotion to Zafira and his familial loyalty, while his power-crazed brother Abdalap commits patricide to usurp the throne. Ultimately, the tragedy maps the cyclic destruction of political greed and bloody betrayals, resolving only when peace is painstakingly restored and the lovers navigate past structural loss to unite their kingdoms.
subject: Theater

title: Kisapmata
creator: Mike de Leon
description: Kisapmata (In the Wink of an Eye) is a 1981 Filipino psychological horror film directed by Mike de Leon, with a screenplay by de Leon, Clodualdo del Mundo Jr., and Raquel Villavicencio, based on National Artist for Literature Nick Joaquin's essay The House on Zapote St. (as Quijano de Manila). It stars Vic Silayan, Charo Santos, Jay Ilagan, and Charito Solis. It tells the story of a controlling patriarch who loses it over his daughter's marriage and her attempts to escape from their incestuous household.
subject: Film

title: Tinimbang Ka Ngunit Kulang
creator: Lino Brocka
description: A privileged teenager (Christopher De Leon) disillusioned with the hypocrisy he witnesses in his small town forms a bond with a lonely leper and a mentally unstable homeless woman.
subject: Film

title: Bayan Ko: Kapit sa Patalim
creator: Lino Brocka
description: In Manila, a printing press employee without social security is forced to participate in a frame-up to pay for his wife's hospitalisation costs following childbirth.
subject: Film
